lead.title: «Κομμένα» κολέγια γίνονται ΑΕΙ εν μία (προεκλογική) νυκτί

items.title: Ζωντανεύουν οι νεκροί;
items.snippet: Η νέα ηγεσία του ΣΥΡΙΖΑ, όποια κι αν είναι, καλείται να αναμετρηθεί με την πραγματικότητα

items.title: Πεσκόφ: Δεν υπάρχει άμεση προοπτική επανέναρξης των ειρηνευτικών συνομιλιών με την Ουκρανία
items.snippet: Σχεδόν 4,5 χρόνια μετά την έναρξη του πολέμου, τα μέτωπα του πολέμου παραμένουν σε μεγάλο βαθμό στατικά, ενώ η Ρωσία και η Ουκρανία χτυπούν η μία τις πόλεις και τις βασικές ενεργειακές υποδομές της άλλης και επιτίθενται σε πλοία στη θάλασσα.

items.title: Πάρος: 23χρονος εμβόλισε μοτοσικλέτα αστυνομικού για να διαφύγει – Δύο συλλήψεις για διακίνηση ναρκωτικών
items.snippet: Δύο 23χρονοι συνελήφθησαν στην Πάρο για διακίνηση ναρκωτικών, μετά από επικίνδυνη καταδίωξη που ξεκίνησε με τον εμβολισμό μοτοσικλέτας αστυνομικού. Κατασχέθηκαν 544,9 γραμμάρια κοκαΐνης και 309,4 γραμμάρια κάνναβης.

items.title: Πέρα από τα οχυρά
items.snippet: Θα μπορέσει η Ουκρανία να κρατήσει τον έλεγχο της μοναδικής της πρόσβασης στη Μαύρη Θάλασσα, της Οδησσού;

items.title: Μαρινάκης για ΟΠΕΚΕΠΕ: Δεν υπάρχει λόγος να μην είναι υποψήφιοι οι τέσσερις που διώκονται [βίντεο]

items.snippet: Ενδέχεται να απελαθούν από τη χώρα.

items.snippet: Η περιοχή βρίσκεται σήμερα σε υψηλό κίνδυνο εκδήλωσης πυρκαγιάς (κατηγορία 3).

lead.title: In Iran war, Trump Risks Another American ‘Forever War’
lead.snippet: President Trump, who promised to “end wars,” not start them, may have fallen into a familiar presidential trap.

items.title: Mass Protests Across Ukraine Oppose Ouster of Defense Minister
items.snippet: Demonstrators demanded the reinstatement of Mykhailo Fedorov, who had come to symbolize Ukraine’s success in using drones to strike back against Russia.

items.title: India Is Moving Fast to Build A.I. Data Centers. A Coastal City May Pay the Price.
items.snippet: With India lagging in the technology, officials are embracing giant data centers. But critics say the megaprojects will use up energy and water, without providing long-term jobs.

items.title: Global Opinion Shifts Toward Favoring China Over the U.S., Poll Finds
items.snippet: An annual survey from the Pew Research Center found that more countries felt positively about China than America.

items.title: Ukraine’s Minister of Defense, a Proponent of Drone Warfare, Is Forced Out
items.snippet: Mykhailo Fedorov announced he was leaving the ministry on Wednesday after conflicts with generals and military contractors over the role of innovative weaponry.

items.title: What to Know About Mykhailo Fedorov, Ukraine’s Ousted Defense Minister
items.snippet: Mr. Fedorov became the youthful face of Ukraine’s successful drone warfare program. The move to replace him has prompted street protests.

items.title: The Quest for ‘Technological Sovereignty’ in Europe (and Why It’s So Hard)
items.snippet: France and Germany want to quit relying on America and China for key technology like artificial intelligence, but they’re having to choose where to do it.

items.title: Sam Neill’s Cause of Death Was Pneumonia, Actor’s Agent Confirms
items.snippet: The beloved New Zealand actor best known for his turn in “Jurassic Park” had “fought and beaten” lymphoma before he fell ill, according to a statement.

items.title: A 59-Year-Old Hunger Striker Joins a Gen Z Protest Movement in India
items.snippet: Sonam Wangchuk, a longtime activist, has fasted for 19 days, joining forces with a student-led campaign to change India’s education system.

items.title: Ukraine Pounds Russian Ships in Its Campaign to Cut Off Crimea
items.snippet: After striking roads and railways, Kyiv is now focusing on sea routes as it tries to disrupt fuel supplies and pressure Russia to end the war.

items.title: He Has a $25 Million Bounty on His Head but Is Also a U.S. Partner in Venezuela
items.snippet: A list of alleged crimes has not prevented Diosdado Cabello, and other Venezuelan officials, from working with a U.S. government that once hounded them.

items.title: France Votes to Allow Assisted Dying
items.snippet: Lawmakers approved a bill that will allow doctors to help terminally ill patients to die, adding France to a short list of nations that allow the practice.

items.title: Wildfires Rage in Ontario as Smoke Affects Toronto’s Air Quality
items.snippet: Air quality in Canada’s largest city was at times the worst in the world on Wednesday, as wildfires across Ontario sent smoke drifting into New York City and beyond.

items.title: Missing From Prince Harry’s UK Trip: A Reunion With Prince William
items.snippet: Harry is clearly repairing his relationship with King Charles III, but the rift with his brother remains unresolved.

items.title: As Argentina Holds Up Falklands Banner on Defeating England, Here’s What to Know About the War
items.snippet: Argentina and Britain fought a bloody war over the islands in 1982. Decades later, the dispute suffuses matches between England and Argentina.

lead.title: Apple Intelligence approved for launch in China with Alibaba and Baidu
lead.snippet: The deal, which was rumored to be in the works last year, marks an important step for Apple's AI ambitions in a key market.

items.title: Ultrahuman’s former hardware VP raises $5.5M for devices that control AI agents, not just record you
items.snippet: Aina is going to pilot a new device in the coming weeks.

items.title: Meta now alerts parents if their teen discussed suicide or self-harm with its AI chatbot
items.snippet: The updates come as Meta and other tech companies are facing scrutiny from regulators and parents around how AI chatbots respond to users in crisis, particularly teenagers.

items.title: Phone maker OnePlus says it won’t release new phones in the US and Europe
items.snippet: OnePlus could also wind down its operations in India by 2027.

items.title: Lululemon backs nylon-recycling startup Syntetica in $30M Series A
items.snippet: Syntetica, a French startup that has developed a novel approach to recycling nylon, has already obtained big-name partners and investors.

items.title: Applied Computing wants to give oil and gas operators an AI model for the entire plant
items.snippet: Applied Computing has raised a $20M Series A to build a foundation AI model for the oil, gas and petrochemical industry.

items.title: Why Greylock capped its new fund at $1.5B when it says it could have raised more
items.snippet: By keeping the number of investments to about 25 per fund, Greylock aims to remain what it calls "the most important partner" to its founders.

items.title: Microsoft is reportedly training salespeople to talk down OpenAI and Anthropic
items.snippet: Microsoft is looking to sell its in-house AI models as more efficient and cost-effective than its competitors' models.

items.title: Tesla driver in fatal Texas crash pressed accelerator 100%, NTSB confirms
items.snippet: The safety board confirmed Tesla's account of the crash, which the company shared days after it happened last month.

items.title: Daniel Ek’s body-scanning startup Neko Health raises another $700M
items.snippet: Neko Health has developed proprietary body-scanning technology, which it couples with bloodwork, to assess a person's health.

items.title: Amid hardware legal battle, OpenAI releases a $230 keyboard for Codex
items.snippet: OpenAI, which is in the middle of a legal battle with Apple over hardware trade theft allegations, just released a light-up keyboard designed to be paired with its agentic coding app.

items.title: Microsoft patches bug in video game Age of Empires II
items.snippet: The vulnerability in the decades-old game could have allowed hackers to take over victims’ computers with a malicious game invite.

items.title: Apple bans home services from its upcoming Maps ads
items.snippet: Apple has published the policies governing its upcoming Maps advertising business, revealing a strategy that differs from Google’s. The new rules prohibit home services businesses like plumbers, electricians, locksmiths, and roofers from advertising on Apple Maps, along with seve

items.title: SpaceX falls to $135 IPO price ahead of Starship launch
items.snippet: The stock has steadily fallen from the euphoric post-IPO high, showing that markets may be sobering up to the promises CEO Elon Musk made before and after SpaceX went public.

items.title: Thinking Machines amps up its bet against one-size-fits-all AI with its first open model, Inkling
items.snippet: It's the company's first public proof point after a year and a half spent building AI infrastructure largely out of public view.

lead.title: Διεκδικούν μετάλλια οι Έλληνες ιστιοπλόοι στο «2026 ILCA 6 Youth European Championship και Open European Trophy»

items.snippet: Η ΑΕΚ έκανε γνωστό στο προεδρείο του ΔΣ του ΕΣΑΚΕ πως στην επόμενη συνάντηση θα κάνει πρόταση για να αυξηθούν οι ξένοι στο ελληνικό πρωτάθλημα από έξι σε επτά.

items.snippet: Η σεισμική ένταση συνεχίζεται στην περιοχή του Μαρμαρά.

items.title: Τσακίρης στους Galacticos by Interwetten: «Όλα τα δεδομένα για Πινέδα και ο σωστός Νίκολιτς»
items.snippet: Ο Γιώργος Τσακίρης στους Galacticos by Interwetten επανέλαβε τη στάση της ΑΕΚ στο θέμα Πινέδα για καταβολή της ρήτρας από τη Μοντερέι των 8 εκατ. ευρώ απευθείας, αλλά και για τις δηλώσεις του Μάρκο Νίκολιτς μετά το φιλικό με τη Φίτεσε.

items.title: Παναθηναϊκός σε Ίνγκασον: «Μαζί σου σε κάθε βήμα στον δρόμο της επιστροφής»
items.snippet: Ο Παναθηναϊκός έκανε μία ανάρτηση στο Instagram για να διατρανώσει στον Ίνγκι Ίνγκασον την στήριξή του στο θέμα του τραυματισμού του.

items.title: Πανταζόπουλος: «Είναι σημαντική μέρα για τον Ηρακλή, έχουμε τη δυνατότητα διάκρισης στον όμιλο»
items.snippet: Ο Πρόεδρος και Διευθύνων Σύμβουλος του Ηρακλή, Γιώργος Πανταζόπουλος, πραγματοποίησε δηλώσεις μετά την κλήρωση του FIBA Europe Cup.

items.title: Προετοιμασία Ολυμπιακού: Η αγωνία των Αργεντίνων του για το Μουντιάλ
items.snippet: Οι χαφ του Ολυμπιακού, όπως και ο Ορτέγα, δεν έχασαν λεπτό από το ματς της Αργεντινής και πανηγύρισαν έξαλλα την πρόκρισή της. ΟΛΛΑΝΔΙΑ, ΑΠΟΣΤΟΛΗ: ΔΗΜΗΤΡΗΣ ΤΟΜΑΡΑΣ.

items.title: Η αμερικάνικη πρεσβεία στην Αθήνα προσλαμβάνει: Ποιες είναι οι θέσεις και ποιοι οι μισθοί
items.snippet: Η διπλωματική αποστολή των ΗΠΑ στην Ελλάδα ανακοίνωσε νέες θέσεις εργασίας για την Αθήνα.

items.title: Παναθηναϊκός: Το απόλυτο σοκ με Ίνγκασον, χάνει τη σεζόν - Χοντρή ζημιά και με Καλάμπρια!

items.title: Έριξαν 120 εκατ. τόνους άμμου στον Ατλαντικό και έχτισαν μια ολόκληρη πόλη (vid)
items.snippet: Πώς μια αφρικανική χώρα έκλεψε χώρο από τη θάλασσα για να χτίσει μια πόλη από το μηδέν.

lead.title: A Spectacular Theodore Roosevelt Library Deep in the Badlands
lead.snippet: An inventive $450 million center devoted to Roosevelt, who died in 1919, blends in with the natural beauty of North Dakota and celebrates conservation.

items.title: Archaeologists Unearth a 2,400-Year-Old Sanctuary for the Worship of Odysseus
items.snippet: The ruins of a 2,400-year-old complex suggest that, to the Greeks of antiquity, Homer’s protagonist was not just a character in a poem but a figure of genuine devotion.

items.title: Finding the Great America, 250 Years On. The Real One.
items.snippet: These history-rich shows at the Smithsonian museums are a culturally-representative accounting of this nation’s history, our critic says. See them before they close.

items.title: Meet Earth’s Newest Monkey. (And Check Out Its Orange Lips.)
items.snippet: It is only the fifth new African monkey identified as a new species in the past 75 years.

items.title: Duchamp Gave Up Art for Chess. At MoMA, He Inspires a New Gambit.
items.snippet: Susan Polgar, a chess grandmaster, will be playing 50 artists, critics and fans of Marcel Duchamp at the same time, in honor of his birthday, July 28.

items.title: New Jersey Couple Aids Meteorite Discovery After it Crashes Through Their Roof
items.snippet: A New Jersey couple helped scientists uncover the makeup of an unusual asteroid after it fell into their bedroom.

items.title: 9 Art Shows to Catch Before They Close This Summer
items.snippet: Among some must-see exhibitions are the Whitney Biennial, a Marcel Duchamp retrospective and a sprawling New Museum survey.

items.title: Canada Wildfire Air Pollution and Heat Wave Combo Pose Heightened Health Risks
items.snippet: Days that are both extremely hot and polluted come with higher risks of respiratory ailments and other health hazards.

items.title: At Hudson Valley Shakespeare, a ‘King Lear’ Worth Traveling For
items.snippet: A beautifully observed version of a man’s final act, along with a lively staging of “As You Like It,” engage the surrounding mountains in conversation.

items.title: New Winged Robot Can Fly and Swim Like a Puffin
items.snippet: Inspired by the physical feats of diving birds, researchers have created a robot that can plunge into the water and flap back into the air.

items.title: ‘Horsegirls’ Review: Finding Her Stride
items.snippet: An autistic woman finds independence by training for a hobbyhorse competition in this sweet-and-sour coming-of-age film.

items.title: Researchers Claim to Have Solved the Perplexing ‘Reverse Sprinkler’ Problem That Stumped Feynman
items.snippet: Physicists have debated which way a submerged sprinkler sucking in water would spin. Careful experiments provide an answer.

items.title: ‘American Pachuco: The Legend of Luis Valdez’ Review: A Rebel With a Cause
items.snippet: The documentary about the stage and screen director is unabashedly celebratory, and leaves you wanting to see more.

items.title: A Sweet Surprise: Scientists Find Sugar Deep in Our Galaxy
items.snippet: It’s the first time a sugar molecule has been detected in interstellar space. The discovery provides tantalizing new clues into how life may have arisen on Earth.

items.title: ‘The Kidnapping of Arabella’ Review: Stolen Childhoods
items.snippet: A disturbed young woman tries to heal her past by abducting a rebellious child in this strange and intermittently touching road movie.
